definition: Something that seems bad at first but turns out to be good
exampleSentence: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise — it led me to a much better career.
exampleTranslation: 失去那份工作因祸得福——它让我找到了更好的职业。

definition: Very common and therefore not valuable
exampleSentence: Smartphones are a dime a dozen these days.
exampleTranslation: 如今智能手机多如牛毛。

definition: Something very easy to do
definitionZh: 小菜一碟；非常容易的事
exampleSentence: The math test was a piece of cake — I finished it in ten minutes.
exampleTranslation: 数学考试非常简单——我十分钟就做完了。

word: a taste of your own medicine
definition: The same bad treatment that you have given to others
definitionZh: 以其人之道还治其人之身
exampleSentence: After years of being rude to everyone, he finally got a taste of his own medicine.
exampleTranslation: 在对所有人粗鲁多年后，他终于自食其果了。

definition: What people do is more important than what they say
exampleSentence: He promises to change every time, but actions speak louder than words.
exampleTranslation: 他每次都承诺会改变，但行动胜于言辞。

definition: To make a bad situation worse
exampleSentence: His sarcastic comment only added fuel to the fire during the argument.
exampleTranslation: 他讽刺的评论在争吵中只是火上浇油。

word: at the drop of a hat
definition: Without any hesitation; immediately
exampleSentence: She'd travel anywhere at the drop of a hat if she could afford it.
exampleTranslation: 如果负担得起，她会毫不犹豫地去任何地方旅行。

definition: To return to the starting point after a failure
exampleSentence: The experiment failed, so we're back to square one.
exampleTranslation: 实验失败了，所以我们又回到了原点。

definition: To be pursuing a wrong course of action or making a false assumption
definitionZh: 找错了对象；搞错了方向
exampleSentence: If you think I took your book, you're barking up the wrong tree.
exampleTranslation: 如果你以为是我拿了你的书，那你找错人了。

definition: To avoid talking about what is important; to speak indirectly
exampleSentence: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what happened.
exampleTranslation: 别拐弯抹角了，告诉我发生了什么。

definition: To try very hard to help or please someone
exampleSentence: The hotel staff bent over backwards to make our stay comfortable.
exampleTranslation: 酒店员工竭尽全力让我们住得舒适。

definition: It is better to do something late than not at all
exampleSentence: You finally started exercising? Well, better late than never!
exampleTranslation: 你终于开始锻炼了？嗯，迟做总比不做好！

word: bite off more than you can chew
phonetic: /baɪt ɒf mɔːr ðæn juː kæn tʃuː/
definition: To take on a task that is too much for you to handle
exampleSentence: By signing up for five courses this semester, I think I bit off more than I can chew.
exampleTranslation: 这学期报了五门课，我觉得我贪多嚼不烂了。

definition: To endure a painful or difficult situation bravely
exampleSentence: I hate going to the dentist, but I just have to bite the bullet.
exampleTranslation: 我讨厌去看牙医，但只能硬着头皮去了。

definition: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance
definitionZh: 祝好运（演出前的祝福语）
exampleSentence: You're going on stage tonight? Break a leg!
exampleTranslation: 你今晚要上台？祝你好运！

definition: To do something innovative that has not been done before
exampleSentence: The research team broke new ground in cancer treatment.
exampleTranslation: 这个研究团队在癌症治疗方面开创了先河。

definition: To do something to reduce tension or awkwardness in a social situation
exampleSentence: He told a joke to break the ice at the beginning of the meeting.
exampleTranslation: 他在会议开始时讲了个笑话来打破僵局。

definition: To destroy relationships or connections so that returning is impossible
exampleSentence: Don't burn bridges with your former employer — you might need a reference.
exampleTranslation: 不要和前雇主断绝关系——你可能需要推荐信。

definition: To work or study late into the night
definitionZh: 开夜车；熬夜学习或工作
exampleSentence: She's been burning the midnight oil studying for her final exams.
exampleTranslation: 她一直在熬夜准备期末考试。

word: by the skin of your teeth
definition: By a very narrow margin; just barely
exampleSentence: I passed the driving test by the skin of my teeth.
exampleTranslation: 我勉强通过了驾驶考试。

definition: To stop working on something for the rest of the day
exampleSentence: We've been working for ten hours — let's call it a day.
exampleTranslation: 我们已经工作了十个小时——今天就到这里吧。

word: cost an arm and a leg
definition: To be extremely expensive
exampleSentence: That designer handbag costs an arm and a leg.
exampleTranslation: 那个名牌手袋贵得离谱。

word: cross that bridge when you come to it
phonetic: /krɒs ðæt brɪdʒ wen juː kʌm tuː ɪt/
definition: To deal with a problem only when it actually happens
definitionZh: 到时候再说；船到桥头自然直
exampleSentence: I'm not going to worry about retirement yet — I'll cross that bridge when I come to it.
exampleTranslation: 我还不打算担心退休的事——到时候再说吧。

definition: To be upset about something that has already happened and cannot be changed
definitionZh: 为已发生的事懊悔；覆水难收
exampleSentence: I know you're disappointed, but there's no use crying over spilt milk.
exampleTranslation: 我知道你很失望，但事已至此，后悔也没用了。

definition: To do something in the cheapest or easiest way, often sacrificing quality
exampleSentence: The builder cut corners on materials, and the roof started leaking.
exampleTranslation: 建筑商在材料上偷工减料，结果屋顶开始漏水。

definition: To get to the main point without wasting time
exampleSentence: Let me cut to the chase — we need to reduce costs by 20%.
exampleTranslation: 我直奔主题吧——我们需要削减20%的成本。

word: don't count your chickens before they hatch
phonetic: /doʊnt kaʊnt jɔːr ˈtʃɪk.ɪnz bɪˈfɔːr ðeɪ hætʃ/
definition: Don't assume something will happen before it actually does
definitionZh: 别高兴得太早；不要过早乐观
exampleSentence: You haven't received the job offer yet, so don't count your chickens before they hatch.
exampleTranslation: 你还没收到录用通知呢，别高兴得太早。

definition: Practical and realistic; not pretentious
exampleSentence: Despite being famous, she's very down to earth and easy to talk to.
exampleTranslation: 尽管很有名，她非常平易近人，很容易相处。

definition: To delay or be slow in doing something because you don't want to do it
exampleSentence: Stop dragging your feet and make a decision already.
exampleTranslation: 别再磨蹭了，赶紧做决定吧。

definition: To make someone very annoyed or irritated
definitionZh: 使某人非常恼怒；把某人逼疯
exampleSentence: The constant noise from the construction is driving me up the wall.
exampleTranslation: 施工的持续噪音快把我逼疯了。

definition: To make a mistake or fail to do something important
exampleSentence: I really dropped the ball by forgetting to send the client the contract.
exampleTranslation: 我忘了把合同发给客户，真是搞砸了。

definition: Something that sounds easy but is actually very difficult to do
exampleSentence: Quitting smoking is easier said than done.
exampleTranslation: 戒烟说起来容易做起来难。

word: every cloud has a silver lining
definition: There is something positive in every bad situation
definitionZh: 否极泰来；每朵乌云都有银边
exampleSentence: I lost my job, but every cloud has a silver lining — I found a better one.
exampleTranslation: 我失去了工作，但否极泰来——我找到了更好的工作。

definition: To accept the unpleasant consequences of your actions
exampleSentence: You made the mistake, and now it's time to face the music.
exampleTranslation: 你犯了错，现在该承担后果了。

definition: To feel slightly ill or not as well as usual
exampleSentence: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today, so I'll stay home.
exampleTranslation: 我今天感觉有点不舒服，所以待在家里。

definition: To become too nervous or frightened to do something you had planned
exampleSentence: He got cold feet right before the wedding and almost called it off.
exampleTranslation: 他在婚礼前临阵退缩，差点取消了婚礼。

definition: To become impossible to control
exampleSentence: The party got out of hand when too many uninvited guests showed up.
exampleTranslation: 当太多不速之客出现时，派对失控了。

definition: To start an activity or process
exampleSentence: Let's get the ball rolling on this project — we don't have much time.
exampleTranslation: 我们开始着手做这个项目吧——时间不多了。

word: give someone the benefit of the doubt
phonetic: /ɡɪv ˈsʌm.wʌn ðə ˈben.ɪ.fɪt əv ðə daʊt/
definition: To believe someone's statement without proof; to trust someone
definitionZh: 姑且信之；给某人一个机会
exampleSentence: He says he was stuck in traffic, so I'll give him the benefit of the doubt.
exampleTranslation: 他说他堵车了，我就姑且相信他吧。

word: go back to the drawing board
definition: To start planning something again because the previous plan failed
exampleSentence: Our proposal was rejected, so we need to go back to the drawing board.
exampleTranslation: 我们的提案被拒绝了，所以需要从头再来。

definition: To make more effort than is expected of you
exampleSentence: She always goes the extra mile to make her customers happy.
exampleTranslation: 她总是不遗余力地让客户满意。

definition: To relax and accept a situation rather than trying to control it
exampleSentence: I didn't have a plan for the trip — I just went with the flow.
exampleTranslation: 我旅行没有什么计划——就顺其自然吧。

definition: To persist through a difficult time; don't give up
exampleSentence: I know the training is tough, but hang in there — it'll be worth it.
exampleTranslation: 我知道训练很艰苦，但坚持住——这是值得的。

definition: To change your mind or feelings about something
exampleSentence: She was going to quit, but she had a change of heart after talking to her manager.
exampleTranslation: 她本来要辞职的，但和经理谈过后改变了主意。

definition: To reach the lowest possible point in a situation
exampleSentence: After losing his job and his apartment, he felt like he had hit rock bottom.
exampleTranslation: 失去工作和公寓后，他觉得自己跌入了谷底。

definition: To study hard, especially for an exam
exampleSentence: I've got finals next week, so I need to hit the books this weekend.
exampleTranslation: 我下周期末考试，所以这个周末需要用功学习。

word: hit the nail on the head
definition: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
definitionZh: 一针见血；说得完全正确
exampleSentence: You hit the nail on the head — the real issue is poor communication.
exampleTranslation: 你说得一针见血——真正的问题是沟通不畅。

definition: In trouble or facing punishment
exampleSentence: He's in hot water with his boss for missing the deadline.
exampleTranslation: 他因为错过截止日期而惹上了老板。

definition: Just in time; at the last possible moment
exampleSentence: The ambulance arrived in the nick of time and saved his life.
exampleTranslation: 救护车及时赶到，救了他的命。

definition: In the same difficult situation as someone else
definitionZh: 处于相同的困境；同病相怜
exampleSentence: We're all in the same boat with these budget cuts.
exampleTranslation: 面对这些预算削减，我们都身处同样的困境。

definition: Both people involved in a situation are responsible for it
exampleSentence: Don't just blame her for the argument — it takes two to tango.
exampleTranslation: 别只怪她引起争吵——一个巴掌拍不响。

definition: It is not very difficult to understand or do
definitionZh: 又不是什么高深莫测的事；没那么难
exampleSentence: Just follow the instructions — it's not rocket science.
exampleTranslation: 按照说明做就行了——又不是什么难事。

definition: To join others in doing something that is currently popular
exampleSentence: Many companies jumped on the bandwagon and started using AI technology.
exampleTranslation: 很多公司赶时髦，开始使用人工智能技术。

definition: To watch or monitor something or someone carefully
exampleSentence: Could you keep an eye on my bag while I go to the restroom?
exampleTranslation: 我去洗手间的时候你能帮我看一下包吗？

definition: To stay positive and hopeful in a difficult situation
exampleSentence: I know things are hard right now, but keep your chin up.
exampleTranslation: 我知道现在很困难，但振作起来。

word: kill two birds with one stone
definition: To accomplish two things at once with a single action
exampleSentence: By biking to work, I kill two birds with one stone — I save money and get exercise.
exampleTranslation: 骑自行车上班一举两得——既省钱又锻炼身体。

definition: To try every possible way to find or achieve something
exampleSentence: The detective left no stone unturned in the search for clues.
exampleTranslation: 侦探在搜寻线索时不遗余力。

definition: To avoid interfering in a situation that might cause problems if disturbed
definitionZh: 别惹是生非；不要自找麻烦
exampleSentence: I know he was wrong, but it's better to let sleeping dogs lie.
exampleTranslation: 我知道他错了，但最好还是别自找麻烦。

word: let the cat out of the bag
phonetic: /let ðə kæt aʊt əv ðə bæɡ/
definition: To accidentally reveal a secret
exampleSentence: I was planning a surprise party, but Tom let the cat out of the bag.
exampleTranslation: 我本来计划办个惊喜派对，但汤姆泄露了秘密。

definition: To tell something briefly without all the details
exampleSentence: To make a long story short, we missed the flight and had to book a new one.
exampleTranslation: 长话短说，我们错过了航班，不得不重新订一个。

definition: To miss an opportunity by being too slow to act
exampleSentence: If you don't apply soon, you'll miss the boat on that scholarship.
exampleTranslation: 如果你不尽快申请，就会错失那个奖学金机会。

definition: You have to work hard and suffer to achieve something worthwhile
definitionZh: 不劳无获；没有付出就没有收获
exampleSentence: Training for the marathon is tough, but no pain no gain.
exampleTranslation: 训练马拉松很辛苦，但不劳无获。

definition: Not something you like or are interested in
exampleSentence: Horror movies are not my cup of tea — I prefer comedies.
exampleTranslation: 恐怖电影不是我的菜——我更喜欢喜剧。

definition: In agreement about something; having the same understanding
exampleSentence: Let's have a quick meeting to make sure we're all on the same page.
exampleTranslation: 我们开个简短的会议，确保大家意见一致。

definition: Very rarely; almost never
exampleSentence: He only visits his hometown once in a blue moon.
exampleTranslation: 他极少回家乡。

definition: Unexpectedly; without warning
exampleSentence: She called me out of the blue after ten years of no contact.
exampleTranslation: 十年没联系后，她突然给我打了电话。

definition: Extremely happy and delighted
exampleSentence: She was over the moon when she found out she got accepted into Harvard.
exampleTranslation: 当她得知被哈佛录取时，她欣喜若狂。

definition: To decide how to deal with a situation as it develops rather than planning ahead
exampleSentence: I don't have a plan for the weekend — let's just play it by ear.
exampleTranslation: 我周末没有计划——我们随机应变吧。

definition: Regular practice or repetition leads to mastery
exampleSentence: Keep playing the piano every day — practice makes perfect.
exampleTranslation: 每天坚持弹钢琴——熟能生巧。

definition: To joke with someone by saying something untrue
exampleSentence: Are you serious, or are you just pulling my leg?
exampleTranslation: 你是认真的，还是在开我玩笑？

word: put all your eggs in one basket
phonetic: /pʊt ɔːl jɔːr eɡz ɪn wʌn ˈbæs.kɪt/
definition: To risk everything on a single plan or opportunity
definitionZh: 孤注一掷；把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里
exampleSentence: Don't put all your eggs in one basket — apply to several companies.
exampleTranslation: 不要孤注一掷——多申请几家公司。

definition: To rain very heavily
exampleSentence: We can't go out now — it's raining cats and dogs.
exampleTranslation: 我们现在不能出去——外面下着倾盆大雨。

definition: To understand the hidden meaning behind what someone says or writes
definitionZh: 领会言外之意；读出弦外之音
exampleSentence: If you read between the lines of his email, he's clearly unhappy with the decision.
exampleTranslation: 如果你读出他邮件的言外之意，他对这个决定显然不满。

definition: To sound familiar; to remind you of something
exampleSentence: The name doesn't ring a bell — are you sure we've met before?
exampleTranslation: 这个名字听起来不耳熟——你确定我们以前见过吗？

definition: A general guideline or practical principle, not a strict rule
exampleSentence: As a rule of thumb, you should save at least 20% of your income.
exampleTranslation: 根据经验法则，你应该至少储蓄收入的20%。

definition: To agree completely with someone
exampleSentence: My sister and I don't always see eye to eye on politics.
exampleTranslation: 我和姐姐在政治上并不总是意见一致。

definition: To not make a decision or take a side in a debate
exampleSentence: You can't sit on the fence forever — eventually you'll have to choose.
exampleTranslation: 你不能永远保持中立——最终你必须做出选择。

definition: To wait until the next day before making an important decision
exampleSentence: It's a big decision — why don't you sleep on it and tell me tomorrow?
exampleTranslation: 这是个重大决定——你不如先想一晚明天再告诉我？

definition: Said when a person you have been talking about suddenly appears
exampleSentence: We were just talking about you — speak of the devil!
exampleTranslation: 我们刚在说你呢——说曹操曹操到！

definition: To reveal secret information unintentionally
exampleSentence: Come on, spill the beans — what did they say about me?
exampleTranslation: 快说吧，告诉我——他们怎么说我的？

definition: To take credit or attention away from someone else
definitionZh: 抢风头；窃取他人的功劳
exampleSentence: She was furious that her colleague stole her thunder by presenting the idea first.
exampleTranslation: 她的同事先提出了那个想法，抢了她的风头，她非常生气。

definition: To politely decline an offer but suggest doing it at a later time
exampleSentence: I'm busy tonight — can I take a rain check on dinner?
exampleTranslation: 我今晚有事——晚餐能改天吗？

word: take it with a grain of salt
phonetic: /teɪk ɪt wɪð ə ɡreɪn əv sɒlt/
definition: To not completely believe something; to be skeptical
exampleSentence: He tends to exaggerate, so take what he says with a grain of salt.
exampleTranslation: 他喜欢夸大其词，所以对他说的话要持保留态度。

word: the ball is in your court
definition: It is your turn to take action or make a decision
definitionZh: 该你行动了；球在你这边
exampleSentence: I've made my offer — the ball is in your court now.
exampleTranslation: 我已经提出了报价——现在该你决定了。

definition: A situation where you can enjoy two different advantages at the same time
exampleSentence: Working from home gives me the best of both worlds — flexibility and productivity.
exampleTranslation: 在家工作让我两全其美——既灵活又高效。

definition: An obvious problem that people avoid talking about
definitionZh: 房间里的大象；显而易见却被回避的问题
exampleSentence: Nobody mentioned the budget deficit — it was the elephant in the room.
exampleTranslation: 没人提到预算赤字——这是大家都回避的显而易见的问题。

definition: The final problem in a series that makes a situation unbearable
definitionZh: 最后一根稻草；忍无可忍的最后一击
exampleSentence: Being late again was the last straw — she decided to fire him.
exampleTranslation: 再次迟到是最后一根稻草——她决定解雇他。

definition: A small visible part of a much larger problem
exampleSentence: The reported cases are just the tip of the iceberg.
exampleTranslation: 已报告的案例只是冰山一角。

definition: Everything; the full extent of something
exampleSentence: They decorated the house with balloons, streamers, banners — the whole nine yards.
exampleTranslation: 他们用气球、彩带、横幅装饰了房子——全套装备都上了。

definition: To think creatively and find unconventional solutions
definitionZh: 跳出思维定式；创新思考
exampleSentence: We need to think outside the box to solve this problem.
exampleTranslation: 我们需要跳出思维定式来解决这个问题。

definition: To give up; to admit defeat
exampleSentence: After months of trying, he finally threw in the towel and closed the business.
exampleTranslation: 尝试了几个月后，他最终认输，关闭了生意。

definition: Time seems to pass very quickly
exampleSentence: I can't believe it's December already — time flies!
exampleTranslation: 不敢相信已经十二月了——时光飞逝！

definition: To deliberately ignore something wrong or illegal
definitionZh: 睁一只眼闭一只眼；视而不见
exampleSentence: The manager turned a blind eye to the employees coming in late.
exampleTranslation: 经理对员工迟到视而不见。

definition: To persuade someone to do something they don't want to do
exampleSentence: I didn't want to go to the party, but my friends twisted my arm.
exampleTranslation: 我本来不想去派对，但朋友们硬是拉我去了。

definition: Feeling slightly ill or unwell
exampleSentence: She's been under the weather all week with a cold.
exampleTranslation: 她整个星期都因为感冒不舒服。

definition: Uncertain; not yet decided
exampleSentence: Our vacation plans are still up in the air.
exampleTranslation: 我们的假期计划还没有确定。

definition: Past events that are no longer important or worth worrying about
exampleSentence: We had our disagreements, but that's all water under the bridge now.
exampleTranslation: 我们之前有分歧，但那都是过去的事了。

definition: Something that will never happen
definitionZh: 太阳从西边出来时；不可能
exampleSentence: He'll clean his room when pigs fly.
exampleTranslation: 等太阳从西边出来他才会打扫房间。

definition: To understand something complicated or surprising
exampleSentence: I still can't wrap my head around how they built that in just one week.
exampleTranslation: 我仍然无法理解他们怎么在一周内就建成了。

word: you can't judge a book by its cover
phonetic: /juː kænt dʒʌdʒ ə bʊk baɪ ɪts ˈkʌv.ər/
definition: You shouldn't judge someone or something based only on appearance
definitionZh: 不能以貌取人；不可以貌取人
exampleSentence: The restaurant looked small and plain, but the food was amazing — you can't judge a book by its cover.
exampleTranslation: 这家餐厅看起来小而朴素，但食物非常棒——不能以貌取人。
